title: Recreation Instruction Classes & Camps
description: The intent of this Request for Proposal (RFP) is to obtain proposals from offerors who can provide programs for classes and camps (summer, spring break, winter break), sponsored by the Town of Herndon Parks and Recreation Department (HPRD). HPRD is interested in attracting offerors willing to provide high quality instruction, personnel, and equipment (using Town of Herndon facilities) at/or below market price to provide a wide variety of affordable programs to residents.

title: VCCS 2025-2026 Small Purchase Procurement for AE Services
description: The Agency often needs professional architectural and/or engineering assistance for small projects such as investigations, studies, reports, design of small projects, and related services. The aim of this RFQ is to identify A/Es interested in providing these services on small projects where the fee will be less than $80,000, in a timely manner, thereby reducing the Agencyâs cost and time for advertising and developing numerous RFPs, and generally to increase the Agencyâs efficiency in obtaining professional services. The purpose and intent of this RFQ is to solicit Statements of Qualifications for any and all such professional architectural and/or professional engineering services.

title: RFP 0815-21-CAC: Camps & Classes
description: Camps & Classes - Recreational Programs. This is an on-going, open RFP with proposals opened, evaluated and awarded periodically. For access to all RFP documents, go to www.fallschurchva.gov/Bids (City of Falls Church Procurement web page). In the event of a conflict between any specific information posted on this website and the specific information in the RFP documents (including addenda) posted on the City's web page, the information and documentation on the Falls Church web page shall take precedence. DO NOT submit any documents or questions via eVA. This posting is an administrative change only to change eVA Solicitation ID from #1294 to #3559.

title: 2026 Virginia Trees for Clean Water (VTCW)
description: The Virginia Trees for Clean Water Grant Program is established to encourage the creation of long-term, sustained canopy cover to improve water quality across the Commonwealth. This grant is used to fund tree-planting efforts that raise public awareness of the benefits of trees and impacts on water quality. Grants may be awarded to civic groups, communities, local government, non-profit organizations, neighborhood associations, public educational institutions, state agencies, tribal organizations, and volunteer groups. Visit the DOF website for more information on qualifying projects, funding amounts, and reimbursable expenses. Applications will be accepted on a rolling basis until December 1, 2026. Applications must be submitted at least 30 days prior to the estimated planting date and an award must be issued prior to beginning the project.

title: A/E Services for Category B Services / RFQ#CB-26
description: The Agency often needs professional architectural/engineering assistance for discrete, small projects such as investigations, studies, reports, design of small projects, and related services. The aim of this RFQ is to solicit statements of interest and qualifications from AE's who will agree to be available to provide these services in a timely manner, thereby reducing the Agency's cost and time for advertising and developing numerous RFQ's & RFP's, and generally to increase the Agencyâs efficiency in obtaining professional services. The qualification criteria shall be re-solicited every year in accordance with the Construction and Professional Services Manual (CPSM).
